---
title: "polyfit"
---


```
p = polyfit(x, y, n=length(x)-1; xscale=1)
```

Returns the coefficients for a polynomial p(x) of degree `n` that is the least-squares best
fit for the data in y. The coefficients in p are in ascending powers, and the length of p is n+1, where

$$p(x) = p[1] + p[2]x + p[3]x^2 + \ldots + p[n+1]x^n$$

The `xscale` parameter is useful when needing to get coeficients in different x units.
For example when converting months or seconds into years.

Examples
--------

Fit a simple linear regression model to a set of discrete 2-D data points.

Create a few vectors of sample data points (x,y). Fit a first degree polynomial to the data.

```{julia}
using GMT

x = 1:50; 
y = -0.3*x + 2*randn(50); 
p = polyfit(x,y,1);

f = polyval(p,x);		# Evaluate the fitted polynomial p at the points in x.
plot(x,y,marker=:circ, legend="data")
plot!(x, f, lc=:brown, legend="linear fit", show=true)
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
using GMT
GMT.doc_source_links("polyfit")
```


See Also
--------

{{< gmtref linearfitxy >}}, {{< gmtref polyval >}}, {{< gmtref plotlinefit >}}, {{< gmtref plot >}}

- [Alphabetical Function List](../alphabetical.html)
